chemicals.name: Calcium Nitrate
chemicals.description: Common nitrogen and calcium fertilizer source.

chemicals.name: Monopotassium Phosphate (MKP)
chemicals.description: Potassium and phosphorus rich fertilizer source.

chemicals.name: Magnesium Sulfate
chemicals.description: Common magnesium and sulfate sulfur source.

chemicals.name: Ammonium Sulfate
chemicals.description: Highly acidic nitrogen and sulfur fertilizer.

chemicals.name: Chlorpyrifos
chemicals.description: Organophosphate insecticide sensitive to alkaline hydrolysis.

chemicals.name: Malathion
chemicals.description: Organophosphate insecticide sensitive to alkaline hydrolysis.

chemicals.name: Imidacloprid
chemicals.description: Neonicotinoid insecticide highly toxic to pollinators.

chemicals.name: 2,4-D
chemicals.description: Selective systemic herbicide for broadleaf weed control.

incompatibilities.reason: Mixing Calcium and Sulfate in concentrated solution forms insoluble Calcium Sulfate (gypsum) precipitation. This clogs sprayer nozzles and renders nutrients unavailable to plants.
incompatibilities.alternative: Apply Calcium Nitrate and Magnesium Sulfate separately or flush lines between applications.

incompatibilities.reason: Calcium and Sulfate react to form Calcium Sulfate precipitate.
incompatibilities.alternative: Replace Ammonium Sulfate with Urea as a nitrogen source, or apply separately.

incompatibilities.reason: Calcium reacts with Phosphate to form insoluble Calcium Phosphate precipitation.
incompatibilities.alternative: Apply Calcium Nitrate separately or use chelated Calcium.

incompatibilities.reason: Calcium reacts with Phosphate to form insoluble Calcium Phosphate precipitation.
incompatibilities.alternative: Apply Potassium via Potassium Nitrate or apply MKP separately.

incompatibilities.reason: Bordeaux Mixture is highly alkaline. Mixing it with Chlorpyrifos (organophosphate) causes rapid hydrolysis, destroying the insecticidal properties of Chlorpyrifos.
incompatibilities.alternative: Apply the fungicide Bordeaux Mixture first, wait 7-10 days, then apply Chlorpyrifos.

incompatibilities.reason: Alkaline nature of Bordeaux Mixture rapidly hydrolyzes and deactivates Malathion.
incompatibilities.alternative: Apply Malathion separately or choose a fungicide with neutral pH like Chlorothalonil.

incompatibilities.reason: Mixing alkaline Bordeaux Mixture with Captan causes chemical breakdown that triggers phytotoxic injury to crop leaves (burning/leaf drop).
incompatibilities.alternative: Use Bordeaux Mixture alone or use Captan with a neutral fungicide.

incompatibilities.reason: Combining sulfur-based compounds like Lime Sulfur with oils (Neem Oil) creates a phytotoxic reaction that severely burns foliage and fruit.
incompatibilities.alternative: Ensure a minimum interval of 14 to 30 days between sulfur and oil applications.

incompatibilities.reason: Mixing Lime Sulfur (calcium polysulfide) with acidic materials like Ammonium Sulfate releases toxic, foul-smelling Hydrogen Sulfide (H2S) gas.
incompatibilities.alternative: Never mix sulfur-based compounds with acidifying agents.

incompatibilities.reason: Combining neonicotinoids (Imidacloprid) with certain fungicides (Captan) can synergistically increase the toxicity of the pesticide to honeybees, posing a high hazard to pollinators.
incompatibilities.alternative: Avoid tank mixing during crop bloom. Apply Imidacloprid late in the evening when bees are not foraging.
